name: Classic Mashed Potatoes (Disney Kids)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the potatoes with cold water in a pot and bring to a boil.
2. Cook for 20 minutes, or until tender. (Watch the pot! Potatoes have a tendency to boil over.)
3. While the potatoes are cooking, slowly heat the milk and butter in a separate saucepan.
4. When the potatoes are done, drain them and add half of the hot milk mixture.
5. Mash the potatoes using a handheld potato masher or an electric mixer until smooth.
6. Keep adding the hot milk gradually until you reach the desired consistency (which may vary from family to family).
7. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg if desired.
8. Serve 4 to 6 people.
VARIATIONS:
- Spuds with Jewels: Briefly saute diced red pepper in vegetable oil with basil, then pour over mashed potatoes.
- Green Potatoes: Blend chopped cooked spinach into mashed potatoes until green.
- Red Coats: Use purple, red, or new potatoes with skins on.
- The Cheddar Broccoli: Mix grated Cheddar cheese and steamed broccoli florets into mashed potatoes.
- Prague Potatoes: Pan-fry bacon until crisp, remove, and crumble it. Top mashed potatoes with bacon, onion, and drippings (using 1.5 teaspoons or less per serving).
- Golden Broil: Spread mashed potatoes in a baking dish, drizzle heavy cream, sprinkle Parmesan cheese, and broil until golden.
- Breakfast for Dinner: Serve mashed potatoes topped with hard-boiled eggs, chopped parsley, and chives.
- Tatties'n'Neeps: Mix equal amounts of mashed potatoes and turnips.
- Colcannon: Add shredded kale or cabbage to mashed potatoes.
- Bangers and Mash: Serve with broiled or pan-seared sausages ("bangers").
NOTES:
This recipe was reviewed in "Vegetables your kids will eat" by Deanna Cook for Family Fun Magazine, May 1996.
